transcript:
Sarah: Hey Priya, Tom — thanks for making time today. How has the last few weeks been?

Priya: Honestly pretty hectic. We've had a lot going on internally. Budget reviews, some team restructuring. It's been a tough quarter.

Sarah: Sorry to hear that. I noticed usage has been a bit lower than usual — wanted to check in and see if there's anything we can do to help.

Tom: Yeah, that's partly on us. We had three people leave the CS team last month and the new hires are still getting up to speed. They haven't been trained on the platform yet so a lot of seats are just sitting unused.

Priya: That's the main thing. I love the idea of what you're building but we're struggling to get the team to actually use it day to day. It feels like a lot of setup for tools they're not sure about yet.

Sarah: That makes total sense. Have you had a chance to look at the workflow templates we sent over — the churn risk one and the expansion detector?

Priya: I actually tried the churn risk one with one of our accounts last week and it was really good. Like, it surfaced stuff I hadn't noticed. But it took me two hours to set it up and I'm not going to be able to do that for 11 CSMs.

Tom: That's the barrier honestly. The output is great when it works. The time to get there is the problem.

Sarah: Totally fair feedback. We're working on a setup wizard that should cut that down significantly — I'll send you a preview. But I hear you — the ROI isn't obvious yet for your team.

Priya: And I'll be honest with you — we have a budget review in May. My VP is going to ask me what we're getting for this spend. Right now I don't have a great answer. That's not a threat, it's just reality.

Sarah: I appreciate you being straight with me. Let's make sure we get you a win before May. What's the account you're most worried about right now?

Priya: Honestly, GlobalEdge. They've gone quiet for two months and their renewal is in July. I've sent three follow-ups and gotten one response. It's a 60k account.

Sarah: Perfect. Let's use that as the test case. I'll set up the churn risk workflow on GlobalEdge with you on the next call — we'll do it live, 30 minutes — and you'll have something real to show your VP.

Tom: That would actually be really helpful. If we can show it works on one account the team will buy in.

Priya: Yeah, let's do that. Can we do Thursday?

Sarah: Thursday works. I'll send an invite with the setup steps in advance so we're ready to go.

Priya: One other thing — we've been looking at Ramp's CS tool. A few people on the team have used it before and they keep asking about it. I'm not saying we're switching but I want to be transparent.

Sarah: I really appreciate that. What's the main thing they like about it?

Priya: Mostly that it's simpler to get started. Less powerful probably but they can just open it and go.

Sarah: Understood. Let's make Thursday a turning point — if we can get GlobalEdge running in 30 minutes I think that changes the conversation.

Priya: Fair enough. See you Thursday.
